message:
feat(stdio/vis): port unvis(3) streaming byte decoder (bd-ftchp)

Adds the NetBSD libutil `unvis(cp, c, astate, flag)` byte-at-a-time
decoder. The pure-safe core machine (`UnvisDecoder`) already
existed; this commit:

- Adds `UnvisDecoder::save_state` / `from_saved_state` so callers
  can round-trip the full state through a single `u32` cell. Layout:
  bits 0..7 = state tag, bit 8 = pending_meta, bits 16..23 = octal
  accumulator. Zero is a fresh decoder.
- Reimplements the abi shim around save/restore (replacing the
  earlier sketch that rebuilt state by replaying synthetic prefix
  bytes — that approach mishandled the `\M-` carry across nested
  escapes).
- Adds 16 abi integration tests: passthrough, caret/double-back-
  slash/octal/meta/meta-caret/meta-octal/meta-named, full 0..=255
  round-trip via the streaming API, SYNBAD on malformed meta and
  malformed octal, NULL-astate guard, NULL-cp tolerance, state
  persistence across calls, UNVIS_END flush behavior on idle and
  partial states.
- Adds 3 core unit tests for save/restore: zero-is-Initial round
  trip, every-open-state round trip with closing byte, full
  alphabet streamed via the save/restore cycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
